Saint Barthélemy

Friends

We've talked about it for years —
and it is finally here.

2027 is bringing us so much to celebrate, and there is no more beautiful place in the world to do it than Saint Barthélemy. We would love for you to join us for a full week in paradise — one we'll be talking about for the next twenty years.

Are you celebrating something special? Let us know when you RSVP — every reason to raise a glass is welcome here.

The Week

We've planned a few special moments to celebrate together—the rest of your time is yours to explore and enjoy.

We'll come together for special celebration events throughout the week, but we encourage you to enjoy the island at your leisure between them. St. Barths is unlike any place you've ever been — pristine beaches, world-class restaurants, hidden coves, watersports, and a pace of life that makes everything else feel far, far away. An entire island of endless adventure, all yours for a week.

At a Glance

Destination
Saint Barthélemy
French West Indies
Duration
One Week
June 25 – July 2
Weather
88°F / 31°C
Warm, tropical & breezy
Documents
Passport Required
Valid US passport needed
Nearest Major Airport
SXM — Princess Juliana, St. Martin
Fly into SXM, then connect via WinAir or St. Barth Commuter to SBH (Gustaf III)

Getting There

1
Fly into SXM
Fly into SXM, then connect via WinAir or St. Barth Commuter to SBH (Gustaf III). If you prefer to skip the small commuter flight, there are high speed ferries (40–60 min) with a short airport shuttle from SXM, although some reviews refer to this ferry as the “vomit comet” due to the rough open seas…
2
Find Your Villa

Saint Barth offers a limited number of small hotels, so many visitors choose to stay in private villas or vacation rentals. Most villas let you customize the number of rooms you rent. There are also some boutique budget hotels scattered across the island with some good internet searching.

We have a villa booked near Anse des Cayes.

Anywhere toward the center of the island is a great location, although if you venture further out you'll likely find cheaper and more exotic rentals with a slightly longer drive to the action. For convenience, we recommend staying in St. Jean, Gustavia, or Grand Cul-de-Sac — and booking as early as possible, as accommodations fill quickly.

Tip: If you're traveling with other guests, consider sharing a villa. It's often more affordable than booking multiple hotel rooms and one of the best ways to experience Saint Barth.

Two great contacts who helped us:

Tracy Cyr
reservations@wimco.com
(401) 236-9022
Cassandre Gombert
cassandre@corcoranstbarth.com

Popular villa rental companies:

  • Wimco Villas
  • Sibarth
  • Airbnb
  • VRBO
3
Getting Around
The island is small but hilly. A rental car, scooter, or ATV is highly recommended. Taxis and private drivers are available but expensive.
4
Join the Celebration
Event details, schedules, and the full itinerary will be shared with all confirmed guests closer to the date. Plan on a day party Sunday, June 27 — you won't want to miss it.
